title: Names
wikitext:
[]: ...Names?             Quote Post by Nipa » Wed Apr 25, 2007 9:33 am ...Is this Asuka's name in kanji...? I thought the characters' names were always written in katakana. The characters for 'Asuka' are also identical to those used for Shinji Wada's Saki Asuka. So, uh... Is this official? (The rest: Misato , Maya , Hikari and Ritsuko )

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Apr 25, 2007 9:37 am These pages from Eve are translated into Chinese. Chinese doesn't have any syllabic systems like hiragana and katakana, so any proper nouns that can't go straight into hanzi* need to be "converted". * The Japanese pilfered these and did SOME streamlining here and there to get kanji.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Apr 25, 2007 3:32 pm Reichu wrote: Chinese doesn't have any syllabic systems like hiragana and katakana.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Thu Apr 26, 2007 4:45 am Actually, to a certain extent, for an experienced Chinese user, he/ she can somehow guess how to pronounce a Chinese character by looking at a particular part of the character. Of course, at most times you can't conjecture the pronounciation as accurate as English, but, Chinese does have a syllabic system.

[]:             Quote Post by Nipa » Thu Apr 26, 2007 5:16 am Ah, so these are just the Chinese translator's guess at the possible way of writing the names? Thanks for the clarification.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Thu Apr 26, 2007 6:06 am Indeed. I've seen two or three Chinese translations for each of the names. But the one you've shown us, I believe they are the most widely accepted versions.

The characters for 'Asuka' are also identical to those used for Shinji Wada's Saki Asuka. So, uh... Is this official? (The rest: Misato , Maya , Hikari and Ritsuko )

Chinese doesn't have any syllabic systems like hiragana and katakana, so any proper nouns that can't go straight into hanzi* need to be "converted". * The Japanese pilfered these and did SOME streamlining here and there to get kanji.

Of course, at most times you can't conjecture the pronounciation as accurate as English, but, Chinese does have a syllabic system.
